mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate held near zero compresses risk-free rates and pushes investors toward equities, producing systematically positive S&P 500 returns over short horizons as long as policy remains accommodative.
failure_condition: Mean 5-day forward return is not significantly greater than zero (t-test p > 0.05) during periods when FEDFUNDS is below 0.25% over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate sustained above 7 percent signals persistent labor market slack, which weakens household income growth and depresses aggregate consumption, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 20-day returns in more than 50 percent of triggered instances when UNRATE exceeds 7.0 percent, or the mean return is not negative.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in industrial production and retail sales captures broad-based demand recovery that single-series signals miss, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The bivariate interaction term fails to outperform the best single-series model in predicting 60-day returns over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC falls below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production levels near the 2017 base index during a post-shock recovery indicate manufacturing normalization that supports earnings expectations, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) in post-shock recovery months when INDPRO is within 2 points of its base index.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts exceeding 850 thousand units signal durable construction demand and positive spillover effects into consumer spending and employment, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 5-day returns are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) when HOUST exceeds 850 over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of elevated unemployment above 7 percent and a federal funds rate below 0.25 percent captures a deeply negative output gap paired with maximum monetary accommodation, forcing capital into equities and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
regime_hint: high unemployment with zero interest rate policy
failure_condition: The interaction term shows a Rank IC below 0.03 over a 2-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in fewer than 55 percent of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained industrial production near the 2017 base index reflects manufacturing stabilization that supports corporate revenue forecasts, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Mean 60-day forward return is negative when INDPRO is between 98 and 102 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP levels below 16 trillion chained 2017 dollars signal a demand shortfall that compresses earnings multiples and weighs on equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 exhibits positive 20-day returns in more than 55 percent of quarters when GDPC1 is below 16000, or the mean return is not negative.
